item.dc:creator: dickblack12

item.title: Old Appalachia
item.description:
Newspaper was often used as wall paper. It kept the draft down.
Many of the houses here are owned by mining companies and rented by the workers.
This guy is making wooden shingles with a maul and fro.
Making wagon wheels
This barefoot boy was searching for coal in mine waste.
This guy is testing the "tops" in a mine
Most cabins were hand made by the residents who lived in them.
Mining community on company owned land
Poverty is rampant in the area even today
This is a Cherokee woman and her daughter
School house in East TN with pot belly stove
Some residents celebrate "Old Christmas" which is on January 6th. A date set before the calendar changed.
Making lye soap
A hanging quilt making frame that can be lifted out of the way when not in use.
Bathing wasn't as common when you had to haul water by hand.
Coal miner breaking ground for a garden
This style of sitting was fairly common. Similar to the eastern European squat.
Prior to people like Saxton Pope and Fred Bear, archery was considered a lost art.
Clearing land for corn
Washing day
Big family, little house
Warming up by the stove
Children working the mines. The smallest were used to operate air shift louvers and drive mules
Popcorn Sutton, famous moonshiner
Factory workers
Sylvester tax
item.dc:creator: wiggyswilderness

item.title: Its time for yet another unhinged XL Wednesday CeleryDump for the good & bad people of Imgur
item.description:
greetings online friends
i promise dude.. there's a bird
mix of chewy/crunchy
mmm hmm
looks somewhat happy
dang
so proud of him
celeryman cares
accurate
relatable
i guess
yes i am a freezer
toadally
called out again
candle unfortunately
now i done it
I only have 4 i guess i don't love the ocean enough
that's my time.. happy new year gang!!
item.dc:creator: CeleryMan7

item.title: Milestones reached. But the journey continues.
item.description:
It's been one year since I last drank alcohol. It was bad before. I was headed down a destructive path. Combination of Luck and Willpower.
I quit smoking in November of 2014. I don't even feel any cravings anymore. And the smell disgusts me. Next up, eat better and move more. Let's see what happens in 2025.
item.dc:creator: JohnnyBoyIsNotAToy

item.title: Stupid people getting rid of the things keeping them safe

item.title: New Year, Same Me
item.description:
Big fan of Halloween, big fan of Nick Castle’s cat
Oops I guess I’m not mature yet
Or Padme AND the dress, for some of us
To be fair, that would be the worst part if I wasn’t aware of oven mitts.
Out of everyone in the world? Me?
My bestie and I discord and craft! I love parallel play with fellow adults.
This is my husband, for sure
Reminder to be kind is not to be a doormat!
This changed my perspective immediately.
My object permanence sucks in many ways.
I watched this around the same age while I had the stomach flu. That was a mistake. Love it now!
And yeah, I chose to get a dog and have a family. But look at the outside world and tell me I just get to do whatever I want lol
And I’m not a man, but otherwise.
*my house
item.dc:creator: clevergrrl420
